items.headline: City Council addresses affordability protections for subsidized housing
items.summary: The council discussed policies to maintain affordability restrictions on housing when project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects the long-term availability of affordable units in San José as federal subsidies wind down.

items.headline: City updates storm sewer master plan with consultant
items.summary: The council approved an amendment to expand the city-wide master planning effort for the storm sewer system with Wood Rodgers, Inc. This planning will guide future infrastructure improvements to manage stormwater across San José.

items.headline: FEMA San José Family Camp project moves forward
items.summary: Council took actions related to the 8721 - FEMA San José Family Camp Project, though specific details are limited. This project involves a federal partnership to support local families.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 construction underway on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four new homes permitted on Altino Boulevard as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development, with construction values ranging from $487K to $573K. This represents a significant residential expansion in the area with multiple units approved simultaneously.

items.headline: Sherly Place housing project approved with three new homes
items.summary: Three new single-family homes permitted at Sherly Place (Lots 2-4), representing buildout of this residential development. The permits show this is a fully entitled project moving into construction phase.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to discuss Mountain View's planned capital spending for the next fiscal year, including infrastructure and development projects. This is worth tracking if you're interested in what physical improvements or construction projects the city is planning.

items.headline: City Council introduces changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is amending its Residential Tenant Protections Program, though the specific changes weren't detailed in the agenda. Residents should monitor this ordinance as it could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in the city.
